sections.heading: 🌅 Pre-Market Overview — 28 May 2026 • 12:48 CET
sections.content:
US futures present mixed signals ahead of the open, with modest divergence across major indices. The S&P 500 futures trade marginally lower at $750.46 (-0.02%) while Nasdaq futures show slight strength at 26675 (+0.07%) and Dow futures lead gains at 50644 (+0.36%). The VIX uptick to 16.63 (+2.09%) reflects emerging caution following overnight geopolitical tensions in the Hormuz Strait.
Key macro crosscurrents include April's hotter-than-expected Fed inflation gauge cementing the steady rate outlook, while WTI crude surged 2.21% to $90.64 on Middle East supply concerns. Gold's retreat to $4430.80 (-1.13%) and Bitcoin's decline to $73408 (-1.24%) suggest risk-off positioning despite equity resilience.

sections.heading: 📊 Index Futures Analysis
sections.content: S&P 500 futures hover near unchanged at $750.46, testing support around the $748-750 zone. Nasdaq outperformance at 26675 reflects continued AI momentum, while Dow strength at 50644 benefits from value rotation. Watch for initial resistance at S&P $752-754 and support at $745-747.

sections.heading: 🎯 Top Pre-Market Movers
sections.content: META leads mega-cap gains at $635.25 (+3.74%) on AI product developments, while AMZN advances 2.47% to $271.85. TSLA climbs 1.56% to $440.36. Conversely, NVDA retreats 1.05% to $212.60 on profit-taking, with AMD down 1.66% to $495.54. JPM weakness at $299.28 (-2.43%) weighs on financials.

sections.heading: 🏦 Sector Rotation & Theme
sections.content:
Communication services and consumer discretionary lead pre-market strength while semiconductors and financials lag. The AI theme remains intact despite chip stock consolidation.
📰 Key Catalysts Today
Fed inflation data analysis continues, while Snowflake earnings momentum and Trade Desk developments merit attention.

sections.heading: ⚠️ Risk Factors
sections.content: Escalating Hormuz tensions could pressure markets, while rate hike speculation may weigh on growth stocks if inflation concerns intensify.
